messages.content: Generate a Streamlit app snippet using plotly.graph_objects only. Assume df already exists. Return code only.

User request:
Visualize a scatter plot of monthly sales against monthly profit, split into separate traces by month where sales > 100 with annotations for added context and include a break-even reference line

Columns in df:
sales: float
profit: float
segment: category
region: category
month: category
order_id: object

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.title("Profit vs. Sales by Month")

df_filtered = df[df["sales"] > 100]

agg = df_filtered.groupby(["month", "month"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#d62728', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd', '#1f77b4', '#2ca02c']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("month", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=12,
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    marker_dict["symbol"] = symbols[i % len(symbols)]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["profit"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["month"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Profit vs. Sales by Month",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Profit ($)",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dash", line_color="red", annotation_text="Break-even")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

User request:
Create a scatter chart comparing total sales vs. total revenue aggregated by year, with one trace per region and include a secondary y-axis overlay and include a margin reference line

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
revenue: float
sales: float
segment: category
region: category
year: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.title("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

agg = df.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#2ca02c', '#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd', '#d62728']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Region",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
x_vals = agg["sales"]
ref_x = [x_vals.min(), x_vals.max()]
ref_y = [v * 0.2 for v in ref_x]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ref_x, y=ref_y, mode="lines", name="20% Margin Reference", line=dict(color="gray", dash="dash")))

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.title("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

agg = df.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#d62728', '#9467bd', '#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#1f77b4']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Region",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

User request:
Show a retail-style dashboard using subplot panels to compare sales and profit by region and region and include a margin reference line

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float

region: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.subheader("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["revenue"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#1f77b4', '#9467bd', '#d62728']
colors_b = ['#98df8a', '#ffbb78', '#ff9896', '#c5b0d5', '#aec7e8']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Revenue",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["revenue"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#fafafa", hovermode="closest")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Show a retail-style dashboard using subplot panels to compare sales and profit by region and region with annotations for added context

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
units: int
revenue: float
region: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["units"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#9467bd', '#ff7f0e', '#1f77b4', '#d62728', '#2ca02c']
colors_b = ['#aec7e8', '#ffbb78', '#98df8a', '#ff9896', '#c5b0d5']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9", hovermode="closest")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.subheader("Profit vs. Sales by Month")

agg = df.groupby(["month", "month"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c', '#9467bd', '#1f77b4', '#d62728']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("month", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=12,
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["profit"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["month"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Profit vs. Sales by Month",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Profit ($)",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dash", line_color="red", annotation_text="Break-even")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    grp_df = agg[agg["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.subheader("Profit and Sales by Segment and Segment")

agg = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)[["profit", "sales"]].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
groups = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
colors_a = ['#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c', '#d62728', '#9467bd']
colors_b = ['#c5b0d5', '#98df8a', '#ffbb78', '#ff9896', '#aec7e8']

fig = go.Figure()

for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    grp_df = agg[agg["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["profit"],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
    ))
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["sales"],
        offsetgroup=i,
        base=grp_df["profit"].fillna(0),
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title="Profit and Sales by Segment and Segment",
    xaxis_title="Segment",
    yaxis_title="Amount ($)",
    yaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="#fafafa",
    paper_bgcolor="#fafafa",
    legend=dict(title="Segment / Metric", orientation="v"),
)
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

    ))
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(

units: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)["units"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["segment"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["segment"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#1f77b4', '#2ca02c', '#d62728', '#9467bd', '#ff7f0e']
colors_b = ['#aec7e8', '#ffbb78', '#ff9896', '#98df8a', '#c5b0d5']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["segment"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["segment"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ex["segment"],
        y=part_ex["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9", hovermode="closest")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.subheader("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

df_filtered = df[df["year"] == 2022]

agg = df_filtered.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#d62728', '#2ca02c', '#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Region",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="#fafafa",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
x_vals = agg["sales"]
ref_x = [x_vals.min(), x_vals.max()]
ref_y = [v * 0.2 for v in ref_x]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ref_x, y=ref_y, mode="lines", name="20% Margin Reference", line=dict(color="gray", dash="dash")))

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a two-panel dashboard combining grouped bars for profit and sales with another supporting view by segment and segment and include a secondary y-axis overlay with annotations for added context

Dataframe columns:
region: category
segment: category

profit: float
units: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.header("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)[["profit", "sales"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)["units"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["segment"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["segment"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#9467bd', '#ff7f0e', '#d62728', '#2ca02c', '#1f77b4']
colors_b = ['#ff9896', '#aec7e8', '#98df8a', '#ffbb78', '#c5b0d5']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["segment"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["segment"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ex["segment"],
        y=part_ex["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#fafafa", hovermode="x unified")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.subheader("Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment")

agg = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)["sales"].sum().reset_index()
overlay = df.groupby("segment", observed=True)["profit"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg["segment"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg["segment"].unique().tolist())
colors = ['#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd', '#1f77b4', '#d62728']
fig = make_subplots(specs=[[{"secondary_y": True}]])

for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    grp_df = agg[agg["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=str(grp),
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["sales"],
        marker_color=colors[i % len(colors)],
    ), secondary_y=False)

overlay = overlay.set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=overlay["segment"],
    y=overlay["profit"],
    name="Total Profit",
    mode="lines+markers",
    line=dict(color="crimson", width=2, dash="dot"),
), secondary_y=True)
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title="Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="#fafafa",
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", secondary_y=False, tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Profit ($)", secondary_y=True, tickformat="$,.0f", showgrid=False)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

selected_year = st.selectbox("Select Year", options_year)

agg = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)["sales"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg["segment"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg["segment"].unique().tolist())
colors = ['#d62728', '#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd', '#1f77b4']

for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    grp_df = agg[agg["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=str(grp),
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["sales"],
        marker_color=colors[i % len(colors)],
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title="Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment",

)

totals = agg.groupby("segment")["sales"].sum()

    fig.add_annotation(x=x_val, y=total_val, text=f"${total_val:,.0f}", showarrow=False, yshift=8)

agg = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)["sales"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg["segment"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg["segment"].unique().tolist())
colors = ['#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd', '#d62728', '#1f77b4']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    grp_df = agg[agg["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=str(grp),
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["sales"],
        marker_color=colors[i % len(colors)],
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title="Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment",
    xaxis_title="Segment",
    yaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5")
totals = agg.groupby("segment")["sales"].sum()
for x_val, total_val in totals.items():
    fig.add_annotation(x=x_val, y=total_val, text=f"${total_val:,.0f}", showarrow=False, yshift=8)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.title("Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment")

agg = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
groups = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
colors_a = ['#d62728', '#1f77b4', '#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd']
colors_b = ['#ff9896', '#98df8a', '#c5b0d5', '#ffbb78', '#aec7e8']

fig = go.Figure()

for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    grp_df = agg[agg["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["sales"],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
    ))
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["profit"],
        offsetgroup=i,
        base=grp_df["sales"].fillna(0),
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="overlay",
    title="Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment",
    xaxis_title="Segment",
    yaxis_title="Amount ($)",
    yaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    paper_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    legend=dict(title="Segment / Metric", orientation="v"),
)
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment")

agg = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
groups = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
colors_a = ['#1f77b4', '#2ca02c', '#9467bd', '#d62728', '#ff7f0e']
colors_b = ['#aec7e8', '#ff9896', '#c5b0d5', '#ffbb78', '#98df8a']

fig = go.Figure()

for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    grp_df = agg[agg["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["sales"],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
    ))
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["profit"],
        offsetgroup=i,
        base=grp_df["sales"].fillna(0),
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title="Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment",
    xaxis_title="Segment",
    yaxis_title="Amount ($)",
    yaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    paper_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    legend=dict(title="Segment / Metric", orientation="h"),
)
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")
totals = agg.groupby("segment")["sales"].sum()
for x_val, total_val in totals.items():
    fig.add_annotation(x=x_val, y=total_val, text=f"${total_val:,.0f}", showarrow=False, yshift=8)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["revenue"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#d62728', '#1f77b4', '#9467bd', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c']
colors_b = ['#aec7e8', '#ffbb78', '#98df8a', '#c5b0d5', '#ff9896']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)

    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(

        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],

User request:
Generate a scatter chart showing the relationship between sales and profit for each month and include a secondary y-axis overlay with annotations for added context and include a break-even reference line

Available columns:
month: category
sales: float
segment: category
profit: float
region: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Profit vs. Sales by Month")

agg = df.groupby(["month", "month"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#d62728', '#9467bd', '#ff7f0e', '#1f77b4', '#2ca02c']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("month", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=12,
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["profit"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["month"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Profit vs. Sales by Month",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Profit ($)",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dash", line_color="red", annotation_text="Break-even")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

User request:
Create a scatter chart comparing total sales vs. total revenue aggregated by year, with one trace per segment where revenue > 10 and include a break-even reference line

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
revenue: float
sales: float
segment: category
region: category
year: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Revenue vs. Sales by Segment")

df_filtered = df[df["revenue"] > 10]

agg = df_filtered.groupby(["segment", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#ff7f0e', '#d62728', '#1f77b4', '#9467bd', '#2ca02c']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("segment", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    marker_dict["symbol"] = symbols[i % len(symbols)]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Segment",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dash", line_color="red", annotation_text="Break-even")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment")

agg = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
groups = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
colors_a = ['#2ca02c', '#9467bd', '#ff7f0e', '#d62728', '#1f77b4']
colors_b = ['#98df8a', '#aec7e8', '#ff9896', '#ffbb78', '#c5b0d5']

fig = go.Figure()

for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    grp_df = agg[agg["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["sales"],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
    ))
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["profit"],
        offsetgroup=i,
        base=grp_df["sales"].fillna(0),
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title="Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment",
    xaxis_title="Segment",
    yaxis_title="Amount ($)",
    yaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="#fafafa",
    paper_bgcolor="#fafafa",
    legend=dict(title="Segment / Metric", orientation="h"),
)
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5")
totals = agg.groupby("segment")["sales"].sum()
for x_val, total_val in totals.items():
    fig.add_annotation(x=x_val, y=total_val, text=f"${total_val:,.0f}", showarrow=False, yshift=8)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

date: datetime

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["units"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#ff7f0e', '#1f77b4', '#9467bd', '#d62728', '#2ca02c']
colors_b = ['#c5b0d5', '#ff9896', '#ffbb78', '#98df8a', '#aec7e8']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#fafafa", hovermode="closest")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a scatter chart comparing total sales vs. total revenue aggregated by year, with one trace per region and include a secondary y-axis overlay and include a margin reference line

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
revenue: float
sales: float
segment: category
region: category
year: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.subheader("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

agg = df.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#1f77b4', '#d62728', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c', '#9467bd']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Region",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
x_vals = agg["sales"]
ref_x = [x_vals.min(), x_vals.max()]
ref_y = [v * 0.2 for v in ref_x]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ref_x, y=ref_y, mode="lines", name="20% Margin Reference", line=dict(color="gray", dash="dash")))

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.subheader("Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment")

agg = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)["sales"].sum().reset_index()
overlay = df.groupby("segment", observed=True)["profit"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg["segment"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg["segment"].unique().tolist())
colors = ['#d62728', '#1f77b4', '#9467bd', '#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e']
fig = make_subplots(specs=[[{"secondary_y": True}]])

for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    grp_df = agg[agg["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=str(grp),
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["sales"],
        marker_color=colors[i % len(colors)],
    ), secondary_y=False)

overlay = overlay.set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=overlay["segment"],
    y=overlay["profit"],
    name="Total Profit",
    mode="lines+markers",
    line=dict(color="crimson", width=2, dash="dot"),
), secondary_y=True)
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title="Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", secondary_y=False, tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Profit ($)", secondary_y=True, tickformat="$,.0f", showgrid=False)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["profit", "sales"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["units"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#1f77b4', '#d62728', '#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd']
colors_b = ['#c5b0d5', '#ff9896', '#98df8a', '#ffbb78', '#aec7e8']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="white", hovermode="closest")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a scatter chart comparing total sales vs. total revenue aggregated by year, with one trace per region and include a secondary y-axis overlay with annotations for added context

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.title("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

agg = df.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#9467bd', '#ff7f0e', '#1f77b4', '#d62728', '#2ca02c']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Region",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee", tickformat="$,.0f")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.header("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["units", "revenue"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["sales"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#2ca02c', '#d62728', '#ff7f0e', '#1f77b4', '#9467bd']
colors_b = ['#ffbb78', '#ff9896', '#98df8a', '#aec7e8', '#c5b0d5']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Revenue",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["revenue"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#fafafa", hovermode="closest")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.header("Performance Dashboard")

df_filtered = df[df["region"] == "West"]

agg_ab = df_filtered.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["revenue", "units"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df_filtered.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["sales"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#2ca02c', '#d62728', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd', '#1f77b4']
colors_b = ['#98df8a', '#ff9896', '#aec7e8', '#c5b0d5', '#ffbb78']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Revenue",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["revenue"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["units"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#fafafa", hovermode="closest")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Revenue vs. Sales by Segment")

agg = df.groupby(["segment", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#2ca02c', '#9467bd', '#ff7f0e', '#1f77b4', '#d62728']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("segment", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Segment",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

Create a scatter chart comparing total sales vs. total revenue aggregated by year, with one trace per region and include a secondary y-axis overlay with annotations for added context

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

agg = df.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#2ca02c', '#9467bd', '#d62728', '#ff7f0e', '#1f77b4']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Region",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

st.title("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

df_filtered = df[df["year"] == 2024]

agg = df_filtered.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#1f77b4', '#d62728', '#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    marker_dict["symbol"] = symbols[i % len(symbols)]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.subheader("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["revenue"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#d62728', '#1f77b4', '#9467bd']
colors_b = ['#98df8a', '#c5b0d5', '#ff9896', '#aec7e8', '#ffbb78']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Revenue",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["revenue"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="white", hovermode="x unified")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

units: int
region: category
revenue: float
profit: float
sales: float
date: datetime

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Performance Dashboard")

df_filtered = df[df["revenue"] > 50]

agg_ab = df_filtered.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df_filtered.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["units"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#1f77b4', '#d62728', '#9467bd']
colors_b = ['#ff9896', '#98df8a', '#ffbb78', '#c5b0d5', '#aec7e8']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9", hovermode="x unified")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a scatter chart comparing total sales vs. total revenue aggregated by year, with one trace per region and include a secondary y-axis overlay and include a margin reference line

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
revenue: float

year: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

agg = df.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#ff7f0e', '#d62728', '#9467bd', '#1f77b4', '#2ca02c']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Region",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5", tickformat="$,.0f")
x_vals = agg["sales"]
ref_x = [x_vals.min(), x_vals.max()]
ref_y = [v * 0.2 for v in ref_x]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ref_x, y=ref_y, mode="lines", name="20% Margin Reference", line=dict(color="gray", dash="dash")))

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

User request:
Create a scatter chart comparing total sales vs. total revenue aggregated by year, with one trace per region and include a margin reference line

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.title("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

agg = df.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#ff7f0e', '#9467bd', '#d62728', '#2ca02c', '#1f77b4']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    marker_dict["symbol"] = symbols[i % len(symbols)]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Region",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="#fafafa",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee", tickformat="$,.0f")
x_vals = agg["sales"]
ref_x = [x_vals.min(), x_vals.max()]
ref_y = [v * 0.2 for v in ref_x]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ref_x, y=ref_y, mode="lines", name="20% Margin Reference", line=dict(color="gray", dash="dash")))

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

Plot a two-panel dashboard combining grouped bars for profit and sales with another supporting view by region and region

Dataframe columns:
profit: float
sales: float
revenue: float
date: datetime
region: category
units: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.header("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["profit", "sales"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["units"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#d62728', '#2ca02c', '#9467bd', '#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e']
colors_b = ['#aec7e8', '#ff9896', '#c5b0d5', '#98df8a', '#ffbb78']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#fafafa", hovermode="x unified")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

messages.content: Generate a Streamlit app snippet using plotly.graph_objects only. Assume df already exists. Return code only.

User request:
Visualize a scatter plot of monthly sales against monthly profit, split into separate traces by month where month == Jun and include a margin reference line

Columns in df:
sales: float
profit: float
segment: category
region: category
month: category
units: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.subheader("Profit vs. Sales by Month")

df_filtered = df[df["month"] == "Jun"]

agg = df_filtered.groupby(["month", "month"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum"), units=("units", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#d62728', '#9467bd', '#1f77b4', '#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("month", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["units"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    marker_dict["symbol"] = symbols[i % len(symbols)]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["profit"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["month"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Profit vs. Sales by Month",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Profit ($)",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")
x_vals = agg["sales"]
ref_x = [x_vals.min(), x_vals.max()]
ref_y = [v * 0.2 for v in ref_x]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ref_x, y=ref_y, mode="lines", name="20% Margin Reference", line=dict(color="gray", dash="dash")))

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.title("Sales vs. Profit by Month")

agg = df.groupby(["month", "month"], observed=True).agg(profit=("profit", "sum"), sales=("sales", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#9467bd', '#d62728', '#1f77b4', '#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("month", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=12,
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["profit"],
        y=grp["sales"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["month"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Sales vs. Profit by Month",
    xaxis_title="Total Profit ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="#fafafa",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dash", line_color="red", annotation_text="Break-even")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

User request:
Create a two-panel dashboard combining grouped bars for profit and sales with another supporting view by segment and segment with annotations for added context

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)[["profit", "sales"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)["profit"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["segment"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["segment"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd', '#d62728', '#2ca02c']
colors_b = ['#ff9896', '#aec7e8', '#ffbb78', '#c5b0d5', '#98df8a']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["segment"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["segment"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ex["segment"],
        y=part_ex["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#fafafa", hovermode="x unified")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Performance Dashboard")

df_filtered = df[df["profit"] > 50]

agg_ab = df_filtered.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df_filtered.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["units"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c', '#1f77b4', '#9467bd', '#d62728']
colors_b = ['#ff9896', '#ffbb78', '#98df8a', '#aec7e8', '#c5b0d5']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9", hovermode="closest")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

Create a two-panel dashboard combining grouped bars for profit and sales with another supporting view by region and region with annotations for added context and include a margin reference line

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
units: int
revenue: float
region: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["profit", "sales"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["revenue"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#2ca02c', '#d62728', '#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd']
colors_b = ['#ffbb78', '#aec7e8', '#ff9896', '#c5b0d5', '#98df8a']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Revenue",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["revenue"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9", hovermode="x unified")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.header("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["units"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())

    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Profit vs. Sales by Month")

df_filtered = df[df["month"] == "Jan"]

agg = df_filtered.groupby(["month", "month"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#1f77b4', '#9467bd', '#d62728', '#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("month", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=12,
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    marker_dict["symbol"] = symbols[i % len(symbols)]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["profit"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["month"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Profit vs. Sales by Month",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Profit ($)",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dash", line_color="red", annotation_text="Break-even")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

agg = df.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c', '#9467bd', '#d62728']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Region",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey", tickformat="$,.0f")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

st.subheader("Profit vs. Sales by Month")

agg = df.groupby(["month", "month"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum"), units=("units", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#9467bd', '#d62728', '#1f77b4', '#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("month", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["units"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),

)

Create a two-panel dashboard combining grouped bars for profit and sales with another supporting view by segment and segment and include a secondary y-axis overlay with annotations for added context and include a break-even reference line

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)[["profit", "sales"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)["profit"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["segment"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["segment"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#1f77b4', '#d62728', '#9467bd', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c']
colors_b = ['#aec7e8', '#ffbb78', '#ff9896', '#c5b0d5', '#98df8a']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["segment"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["segment"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ex["segment"],
        y=part_ex["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="white", hovermode="x unified")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

Generate a retail-style dashboard using subplot panels to compare units and revenue by region and region and include a secondary y-axis overlay and include a break-even reference line

Available columns:
profit: float
revenue: float
date: datetime
sales: float
region: category
units: int

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["units", "revenue"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["sales"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#1f77b4', '#2ca02c', '#d62728', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd']
colors_b = ['#aec7e8', '#ff9896', '#ffbb78', '#98df8a', '#c5b0d5']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Revenue",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["revenue"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="white", hovermode="closest")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.subheader("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)[["units", "revenue"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["region", "region"], observed=True)["profit"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["region"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#d62728', '#2ca02c', '#1f77b4', '#9467bd', '#ff7f0e']
colors_b = ['#aec7e8', '#98df8a', '#c5b0d5', '#ffbb78', '#ff9896']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["region"] == grp].set_index("region").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Revenue",
        x=part_ab["region"],
        y=part_ab["revenue"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ex["region"],
        y=part_ex["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="#fafafa", hovermode="x unified")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment")

agg = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
groups = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
colors_a = ['#d62728', '#1f77b4', '#2ca02c', '#9467bd', '#ff7f0e']
colors_b = ['#ff9896', '#ffbb78', '#aec7e8', '#c5b0d5', '#98df8a']

fig = go.Figure()

for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    grp_df = agg[agg["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["sales"],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
    ))
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=grp_df["segment"],
        y=grp_df["profit"],
        offsetgroup=i,
        base=grp_df["sales"].fillna(0),
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    title="Sales and Profit by Segment and Segment",
    xaxis_title="Segment",
    yaxis_title="Amount ($)",
    yaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    paper_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    legend=dict(title="Segment / Metric", orientation="h"),
)
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey")
totals = agg.groupby("segment")["sales"].sum()
for x_val, total_val in totals.items():
    fig.add_annotation(x=x_val, y=total_val, text=f"${total_val:,.0f}", showarrow=False, yshift=8)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.header("Revenue vs. Sales by Region")

agg = df.groupby(["region", "year"], observed=True).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), revenue=("revenue", "sum"), year=("year", "sum")).reset_index()
colors = ['#d62728', '#9467bd', '#2ca02c', '#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e']
symbols = ['circle', 'diamond', 'square', 'x', 'triangle-up']
fig = go.Figure()

for i, (grp_name, grp) in enumerate(agg.groupby("region", observed=True)):
    marker_dict = dict(
        size=grp["year"],
        color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        opacity=0.82,
        line=dict(width=1, color="white"),
    )
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=grp["sales"],
        y=grp["revenue"],
        mode="markers",
        name=str(grp_name),
        text=grp["year"],
        marker=marker_dict,
    ))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Revenue vs. Sales by Region",
    xaxis_title="Total Sales ($)",
    yaxis_title="Total Revenue ($)",
    hovermode="closest",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee", tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee", tickformat="$,.0f")
x_vals = agg["sales"]
ref_x = [x_vals.min(), x_vals.max()]
ref_y = [v * 0.2 for v in ref_x]
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ref_x, y=ref_y, mode="lines", name="20% Margin Reference", line=dict(color="gray", dash="dash")))

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.subheader("Performance Dashboard")

agg_ab = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
agg_extra = df.groupby(["segment", "segment"], observed=True)["units"].sum().reset_index()
x_vals = sorted(agg_ab["segment"].unique().tolist())
groups = sorted(agg_ab["segment"].unique().tolist())
colors_a = ['#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#9467bd', '#d62728', '#2ca02c']
colors_b = ['#ffbb78', '#ff9896', '#98df8a', '#c5b0d5', '#aec7e8']
fig = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2, subplot_titles=("Metric Comparison", "Supporting View"), shared_yaxes=False)
for i, grp in enumerate(groups):
    part_ab = agg_ab[agg_ab["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    part_ex = agg_extra[agg_extra["segment"] == grp].set_index("segment").reindex(x_vals).fillna(0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Sales",
        x=part_ab["segment"],
        y=part_ab["sales"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Profit",
        x=part_ab["segment"],
        y=part_ab["profit"],
        marker_color=colors_b[i % len(colors_b)],
        offsetgroup=i,
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=1)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=f"{grp} — Units",
        x=part_ex["segment"],
        y=part_ex["units"],
        marker_color=colors_a[i % len(colors_a)],
        legendgroup=str(grp),
        showlegend=False,
    ), row=1, col=2)
fig.update_layout(barmode="group", title="Performance Dashboard", plot_bgcolor="white", hovermode="closest")

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=False)
